summary:
GnRH agonists treat PCOS by suppressing excessive LH secretion, thereby reducing adrenal and ovarian androgen production. Although primarily indicated for PCOS, they are also commonly used in prostate cancer to lower testosterone levels through similar hormonal mechanisms.  

(Note: This summary focuses solely on clinically relevant pharmacological actions related to the query.) 

Final Answer: D
